project: Adult Player Lifecycle & Daily Health Check
description: Extends PlayerARC from guardian-managed youth players to full adult self-management. 7 phases, 42 stories. Covers: (1) player portal layout & sidebar, (2) youth-to-adult 18th birthday transition, (3) adult import with youth record matching, (4) 8-dimension daily wellness check with per-coach consent and offline support, (5) full portal remaining sections including coach feedback & AI summaries, (6) multi-role UX for users with 2+ roles, (7) child player passport authorization with age-gated access levels. See individual phase JSON files in the AdultPlayerLifeCycle/ folder for full story detail.
readinessLevel: 100% - Analysis complete, existing groundwork documented. Phase JSON files are the authoritative implementation guide.

criticalContext: EXTEND, NEVER REBUILD: Significant groundwork already exists — see keyFiles below

Player portal already exists at apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/page.tsx (full single-page dashboard with passport, emergency contacts, benchmarks, goals, skills). Phase 1 adds layout.tsx + sidebar AROUND it — do NOT replace content
'player' functional role already in org-role-switcher.tsx, routes to /orgs/[orgId]/player

Graduation backend fully built: playerGraduations table, playerClaimTokens, getPendingGraduations, sendGraduationInvite, dismissGraduationPrompt, getPlayerClaimStatus, claimPlayerAccount — all in playerGraduations.ts

Adult player mutations all built: transitionToAdult (line 206), claimYouthProfile (line 350), registerAdultPlayer (line 147), getPlayerDashboard (line 503), hasPlayerDashboard (line 633) — all in adultPlayers.ts

KNOWN GAP: Graduation invitation email sending is TODO at playerGraduations.ts:217 — Phase 2 (US-P-005) completes this by adding sendGraduationInvitationEmail() to packages/backend/convex/utils/email.ts
Onboarding orchestrator has player_graduation task type already wired at apps/web/src/components/onboarding/onboarding-orchestrator.tsx — just needs player-graduation-step.tsx component
Email via Resend: packages/backend/convex/utils/email.ts, from team@notifications.playerarc.io, uses RESEND_API_KEY env var — follow exact same pattern for graduation email
Parent portal at apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/parents/ is the template to mirror for player portal layout and sidebar
Player import flow at admin/player-import/page.tsx is authoritative — EXTEND with match badges, do NOT create a new import pipeline
Guardian multi-signal matching algorithm at playerImport.ts:148 — reuse scoring approach for youth-to-adult matching
gender field on playerIdentities: v.union(v.literal('male'), v.literal('female'), v.literal('other')) — used for cycle tracking visibility
NEW TABLES NEEDED (Phase 4): dailyPlayerHealthChecks, playerWellnessSettings, wellnessCoachAccess, playerHealthConsents — add all 4 to packages/backend/convex/schema.ts

Phase 4 wellness: 8 dimensions (sleepQuality, energyLevel, foodIntake, waterIntake, mood, motivation, physicalFeeling, muscleRecovery), 5-point emoji scale, per-coach consent model (wellnessCoachAccess table), offline IndexedDB support. Coaches see ONLY aggregate scores — never individual dimensions.
NEW TABLE NEEDED (Phase 7): parentChildAuthorizations — unified access record per child (not per parent), access levels none/view_only/view_interact, granular content toggles, audit changeLog
Phase 6 (Multi-Role): extends existing org-role-switcher.tsx, adds primary role setting, role context badge, self-registration as player
Phase 7 (Child Authorization): parent grants child age-gated access (13+), child_account_setup route, restrictChildView field on notes, 30/7 day pre-birthday notifications extending graduation cron
Cycle phase data is GDPR Article 9 Special Category health data — requires separate explicit consent, 18-month max retention, access-controlled (player + medical admin only, NOT coaches)
BetterAuth tables (user, member, organization, team): use ctx.runQuery(components.betterAuth.adapter.findMany/findOne). Application tables: use ctx.db directly. Never mix.
Always use .withIndex() — NEVER .filter() in Convex. Batch fetch with Map, never N+1 loops.
All queries and mutations MUST include returns validator
keyFiles: packages/backend/convex/models/adultPlayers.ts — all adult player mutations/queries (READ BEFORE STARTING)
packages/backend/convex/models/playerImport.ts:148 — matching algorithm to reuse for findMatchingYouthProfile
packages/backend/convex/models/notifications.ts — notification type pattern (add age_transition_available, age_transition_claimed, wellness_alert)
packages/backend/convex/utils/email.ts — Resend email utility to extend with sendGraduationInvitationEmail()
packages/backend/convex/schema.ts — add dailyPlayerHealthChecks, playerWellnessSettings, wellnessCoachAccess, playerHealthConsents (Phase 4) and parentChildAuthorizations (Phase 7) tables
apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/parents/components/ — sidebar and component patterns to mirror
apps/web/src/components/onboarding/onboarding-orchestrator.tsx — has player_graduation task type, needs player-graduation-step.tsx

criticalPatterns.ExtendNotRebuild: All three entry points (manual add, CSV import, email invite) already exist. Add matching logic to them, do NOT create new flows.
criticalPatterns.PlayerPortalLayout: Add apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/layout.tsx that wraps existing page.tsx. The existing page content becomes the Overview tab — remove nothing.
criticalPatterns.GraduationEmail: Add sendGraduationInvitationEmail() to packages/backend/convex/utils/email.ts. Copy the fetch/Resend pattern exactly from existing functions. This completes the TODO at playerGraduations.ts:217.
criticalPatterns.OnboardingOrchestrator: Create apps/web/src/components/onboarding/player-graduation-step.tsx as a new step component. The orchestrator already has player_graduation task type — just wire it to this component.
criticalPatterns.CyclePhasGDPR: Cycle phase section must NEVER appear without prior GDPR consent. First render intercepts with consent modal. Store consent in playerHealthConsents table. Coaches NEVER see cycle data.
criticalPatterns.WellnessVisibility: Player sees own data. Parent sees child data only while child < 18. Coach sees aggregate wellness score only (NO cycle phase). Admin/medical sees all including cycle phase.
criticalPatterns.YouthMatchingAlgorithm: findMatchingYouthProfile: exact DOB + surname = HIGH confidence; DOB + first name = MEDIUM; surname only = LOW. HIGH = blocking dialog. MEDIUM = amber warning.
criticalPatterns.N1Prevention: All analytics queries (wellness, matching) must batch fetch. No queries inside loops.
criticalPatterns.IndexUsage: dailyPlayerHealthChecks: use by_player_and_date for single-player queries, by_org_and_date for org-wide, by_player for history. Always withIndex().
criticalPatterns.ReturnValidators: Every query and mutation must have a returns: validator — no exceptions.

userStories.title: Player Portal Layout & Sidebar Navigation
userStories.description: As an adult player, I want a sidebar-navigated portal matching the depth of the parent portal, so that I can access all my sports management features in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/layout.tsx — this file does NOT currently exist
Build PlayerSidebar component by mirroring ParentSidebar at apps/web/src/components/layout/parent-sidebar.tsx
Existing player/page.tsx content must remain completely intact — it becomes the Overview tab, just framed in the new layout
hasPlayerDashboard query (adultPlayers.ts:633) gates the entire portal — redirect to org home if user has no player profile

Sidebar navigation items (9 total): Overview (Home icon, /player/), My Profile (User icon, /player/profile), My Progress (TrendingUp icon, /player/progress), My Teams (Users icon, /player/teams), Daily Wellness (Heart icon, /player/health-check), My Injuries (Activity icon, /player/injuries), Coach Feedback (MessageSquare icon, /player/feedback), Passport Sharing (Share2 icon, /player/sharing), Settings (Settings icon, /player/settings)
Mobile responsive: bottom nav tabs (4 primary items) matching parent portal pattern at 375px minimum
Org theming via useOrgTheme() applied consistently — CSS vars --org-primary, --org-secondary, --org-tertiary
Sub-routes that don't have pages yet (progress, teams, injuries, sharing, health-check, feedback, settings) show a placeholder 'Coming Soon' page — no 404 errors
npm run check-types passes
npx -w packages/backend convex codegen passes
userStories.notes: Read apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/parents/layout.tsx and apps/web/src/components/layout/parent-sidebar.tsx before starting. Mirror the pattern exactly. The player/page.tsx is a full dashboard — do not touch its content.

userStories.title: Player Overview Dashboard Enhancement with Wellness CTA
userStories.description: As an adult player, I want the existing overview to surface a Daily Wellness CTA and key summary cards, so that my most important actions are immediately visible.
acceptanceCriteria: All existing content in player/page.tsx (passport, emergency contacts, benchmarks, goals, skills, positions) remains intact
Add a summary card row at the TOP of the existing page: active injuries count card, latest assessment rating card, today's wellness status card
If wellness check not yet submitted today: show amber 'Complete Your Daily Wellness Check' CTA card linking to /player/health-check
If wellness check already submitted today: show green 'Wellness checked in today ✓' status card
All summary cards show empty state (with appropriate icon and message) when no data exists
Summary data fetched via existing getPlayerDashboard(organizationId) query — no new backend query needed for the card row

Today's wellness status requires a new lightweight query getTodayHealthCheck(playerIdentityId, date) — add to a new file packages/backend/convex/models/playerHealthChecks.ts (Phase 4 will expand this file)
Mobile-first card layout: stack vertically on mobile, 3-column grid on desktop
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Read the existing player/page.tsx fully before touching it. Add the summary row above the existing content, don't replace anything.

userStories.title: Player Profile Self-Edit Sub-Page
userStories.description: As an adult player, I want a dedicated page to manage my contact details and emergency contacts, so that my information stays current without asking an admin.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/profile/page.tsx
Editable fields (using updateMyProfile mutation at adultPlayers.ts:287): email, phone, address, town, postcode, country
Read-only fields: firstName, lastName, dateOfBirth, gender — each shown with a Lock icon and tooltip 'Contact your admin to change'
Form uses React Hook Form with Zod validation, matching existing form patterns in the codebase
Save button shows loading state during mutation, success toast on save, error toast on failure
Emergency contacts section below the personal details form
Emergency contacts section: display all existing emergency contacts (fetched via getMyPlayerProfile query) with name, phone, relationship
Emergency contacts section: Add button opens a dialog to create new emergency contact
Emergency contacts section: Edit and Delete actions on each contact row
Emergency contact mutations: add to packages/backend/convex/models/adultPlayers.ts following the playerEmergencyContacts pattern already established
Mobile-first layout: 375px minimum width, single-column on mobile
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: updateMyProfile mutation is already built at adultPlayers.ts:287. getMyPlayerProfile query returns both player and emergencyContacts. Emergency contact CRUD mutations need to be added.

Test: user without player role visiting /orgs/[orgId]/player is redirected to org home

Test: read-only fields (name, DOB) show lock icon and form inputs are disabled

Manual test 2: Log in as user WITHOUT player role → confirm redirect to org home
Manual test 3: Navigate to My Profile → edit phone number → save → refresh → confirm new number persists

userStories.title: Guardian Sees Graduation Alert in Parent Dashboard
userStories.description: As a parent/guardian, I want to see a clear prompt when my child turns 18, so that I can initiate the handover of their account.
acceptanceCriteria: Add graduation alert banner/card to the parent dashboard — follow the action-items-panel.tsx pattern already used for pending actions in the parent portal
Alert driven by getPendingGraduations() query (already built in playerGraduations.ts)
Alert shows: player full name, date of birth, org name, 'They turned 18 on [date formatted as DD MMM YYYY]'
Two action buttons on the alert: 'Send Account Invite' (opens email dialog from US-P-005) and 'Dismiss'
'Dismiss' calls dismissGraduationPrompt mutation (already built) and hides the banner immediately
Add notification type 'age_transition_available' to the type union in packages/backend/convex/models/notifications.ts
When graduation record is created (by the daily cron in graduations.ts), send an in-app notification of type age_transition_available to the guardian
Banner persists across page refreshes until dismissed or player claims their account (graduation status = 'claimed')
If multiple children have pending graduations, show one alert per child
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Read apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/parents/ directory to find the right place to inject the banner. The action-items-panel.tsx is the pattern to follow. getPendingGraduations is already built in playerGraduations.ts.

userStories.title: Guardian Sends Account Invite Email to Player
userStories.description: As a parent/guardian, I want to send my child a secure email link to claim their account, so that they can take control of their sports profile.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Send Account Invite' from US-P-004 alert opens a dialog
Dialog pre-fills player's email address from any existing data on the playerIdentity record (editable by guardian)
Dialog shows player name and a brief explanation of what the email will do
On confirm: calls sendGraduationInvite(playerIdentityId, playerEmail) mutation (already built in playerGraduations.ts)
COMPLETE THE TODO at playerGraduations.ts:217: the sendGraduationInvite mutation must trigger a Convex action that calls sendGraduationInvitationEmail()
Add sendGraduationInvitationEmail() to packages/backend/convex/utils/email.ts — copy the exact Resend fetch pattern from existing functions (see sendOrganizationInvitation as template)
Email template includes: player first name, org name, 'Claim Your Account' CTA button, token link to /claim-account?token=xxx, '30-day expiry' warning, PlayerARC branding
Email sent from 'PlayerARC <team@notifications.playerarc.io>'
After successful send: guardian sees confirmation message 'Invite sent to [email]. Link valid for 30 days.'

If guardian sends again (token expired or resend): sendGraduationInvite creates new token, previous token automatically invalidated (already handled in existing mutation logic — verify this is the case)
npm run check-types passes
npx -w packages/backend convex codegen passes
userStories.notes: This is the critical TODO completion. The mutation sendGraduationInvite already exists and creates the token. It just needs to trigger an action for email sending. Read packages/backend/convex/utils/email.ts fully before writing the new function. Actions go in packages/backend/convex/actions/ directory.

userStories.title: Player Claims Account via Token & Onboards via Orchestrator
userStories.description: As a player turning 18, I want to click my invite link, claim my sports profile, and be guided through setup, so that I can manage my own development journey.
acceptanceCriteria: Create public route apps/web/src/app/claim-account/page.tsx that accepts ?token=xxx query parameter
Page calls getPlayerClaimStatus(token) (already built in playerGraduations.ts) to validate token
Valid token: show player name, org name, and 'Claim [Name's] profile at [Org]?' confirmation UI
Invalid token: show 'This link is invalid' error with 'Contact your guardian for a new invite' message
Expired token: show 'This invite link has expired' with 'Ask your guardian to resend the invite' message
Already-used token: show 'This account has already been claimed. Try signing in.' message
If user not logged in: show sign-in/sign-up prompt, preserve token in URL params through auth redirect
If user logged in and token valid: show confirmation dialog before claiming
On confirm: calls claimPlayerAccount(token, userId) (already built in playerGraduations.ts)
After successful claim: redirect to /orgs/[orgId] — the existing onboarding orchestrator handles next steps via player_graduation task type
Create apps/web/src/components/onboarding/player-graduation-step.tsx component to implement the player_graduation task in the orchestrator
player-graduation-step.tsx shows: welcome message with player name, explanation of the player portal, 'Go to My Dashboard' button linking to /orgs/[orgId]/player
Wire player-graduation-step.tsx into the orchestrator's task type switch — follow the pattern of unified-guardian-claim-step.tsx
Add notification type 'age_transition_claimed' to notifications.ts type union — send to org admins when a player claims their account
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Read apps/web/src/components/onboarding/onboarding-orchestrator.tsx fully — player_graduation task type is already there, just needs the step component. Read unified-guardian-claim-step.tsx as the pattern to follow for the new step component.

userStories.title: Admin Manual Graduation Trigger
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to manually trigger the graduation flow for a player, so that I can handle edge cases where the guardian isn't responding.
acceptanceCriteria: In the player profile admin view (/admin/players/[playerId]), add a 'Graduation' section that appears only when: player age >= 18 AND playerType === 'youth'
Graduation section shows the current graduation status (pending / invitation_sent / claimed / dismissed) from the playerGraduations table
If status is 'pending' or 'dismissed': admin can click 'Send Invitation' to send email directly to player (bypassing guardian) — uses the same sendGraduationInvite flow from US-P-005
Admin can click 'Transition Now' to call transitionToAdult(playerIdentityId) without requiring a token — this immediately converts the player to adult type
'Transition Now' shows a confirmation dialog: 'This will convert [Name] to an adult player. Guardian contacts will be converted to emergency contacts. This cannot be undone. Proceed?'
Status updates in real-time via Convex reactive query
If player profile admin view page doesn't exist yet at /admin/players/[playerId], create it as a new page showing player details
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Check if /admin/players/[playerId] route exists. If not, create a basic player detail page. The transitionToAdult mutation is already built at adultPlayers.ts:206.

acceptanceCriteria: Create test file: apps/web/uat/tests/player-graduation-phase2.spec.ts
Test: graduation alert appears in parent dashboard for a player with graduation status 'pending'
Test: dismiss button removes the graduation alert
Test: /claim-account?token=xxx with valid token shows player name and org
Test: /claim-account?token=invalid shows invalid token error state
Test: /claim-account with expired token shows expired error state
Test: /claim-account with already-used token shows already-claimed state
Test: after successful claim, onboarding orchestrator shows player_graduation step
Manual test 1: Create test player with DOB exactly 18 years ago → run graduation cron manually → confirm playerGraduations record with status 'pending'
Manual test 2: Log in as the player's guardian → confirm graduation alert banner in parent dashboard
Manual test 3: Click 'Send Account Invite' → enter email → confirm Resend email dispatched (check Convex logs)
Manual test 4: Click token link from email → confirm claim page shows player name and org
Manual test 5: Sign in as player → complete claim → confirm redirect to onboarding → complete player_graduation step → land in player portal
Manual test 6: Attempt to use same token again → confirm 'already used' error
Manual test 7: Manually set expiresAt to past in DB → confirm 'expired' message
Manual test 8: As admin → navigate to youth player aged 18+ → confirm Graduation section visible → trigger manual transition → confirm playerType changes to 'adult'
userStories.notes: The graduation cron can be triggered manually via Convex dashboard. Use the Convex MCP tools to inspect playerGraduations records during testing.

userStories.title: Backend: findMatchingYouthProfile Query
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need a reusable query that checks if an adult being added matches an existing youth playerIdentity, so that all three entry points (manual add, CSV import, email invite) can use the same matching logic.
userStories.phase: Phase 3: Adult Import & Youth Record Matching
acceptanceCriteria: Add findMatchingYouthProfile query to packages/backend/convex/models/playerImport.ts (or a new file packages/backend/convex/models/playerMatching.ts if more appropriate)
Args: organizationId (string), firstName (string), lastName (string), dateOfBirth (string), email (optional string)
Query only playerIdentities where playerType === 'youth' using .withIndex()
Matching algorithm: exact DOB + exact surname (case-insensitive) → HIGH confidence; exact DOB + firstName match → MEDIUM confidence; surname only → LOW confidence
If email provided and matches: boost confidence by one level (LOW→MEDIUM, MEDIUM→HIGH)
Return type: { confidence: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low' | 'none', match: playerIdentityValidator | null, matchedFields: string[] }
Include returns validator with the full return shape
Do NOT query inside a loop — this query is called once per candidate, not per org player
npm run check-types passes
npx -w packages/backend convex codegen passes
userStories.notes: Read playerImport.ts:148 to understand the existing scoring pattern. Reuse the approach. This single query is the foundation for all three Phase 3 stories (US-P-008-UI, US-P-009, US-P-010).

userStories.title: Youth Record Matching on Manual Add Player Form
userStories.description: As an admin adding an adult player manually, I want the system to suggest if this adult matches an existing youth record, so that I don't create duplicate profiles and lose their history.
userStories.phase: Phase 3: Adult Import & Youth Record Matching
acceptanceCriteria: EXTEND apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/admin/players/page.tsx — do NOT replace or duplicate the existing Add Player form
When admin submits the form for a player with calculated age >= 18: run findMatchingYouthProfile in parallel with the existing duplicate check
HIGH confidence match: show blocking dialog 'A youth profile matching this player exists — [Name], born [DOB]. Link to existing history or create new?'
HIGH confidence dialog has two options: 'Link to Existing History' (calls transitionToAdult on the matched playerIdentityId) and 'Create New Profile' (existing flow unchanged)
MEDIUM confidence match: show non-blocking amber warning banner above the form 'A youth profile may match this player. Review before proceeding.' with a 'View Match' link
LOW confidence or no match: proceed with existing flow unchanged
'Link to Existing History' action: does NOT create a new playerIdentity — calls transitionToAdult(existingPlayerIdentityId) and shows success 'Player profile updated to adult and linked'
All existing duplicate detection logic (same name + DOB + gender = exact duplicate) remains unchanged and runs first
Mobile responsive: dialog works at 375px width
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Read the existing admin/players/page.tsx fully before touching it. The existing duplicate check must still run. This is an additive extension for adult players only.

userStories.title: Youth Record Matching on CSV Import
userStories.description: As an admin running a player import, I want adult players in the CSV to be flagged against existing youth records, so that imported data enriches existing profiles rather than duplicating them.
userStories.phase: Phase 3: Adult Import & Youth Record Matching
acceptanceCriteria: EXTEND the existing import flow at apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/admin/player-import/page.tsx — do NOT create a new import pipeline
In the import preview/review table, add a new column 'Youth Match' that appears only for rows where the player is aged >= 18
For each adult row: run findMatchingYouthProfile and show a confidence badge: 'High' (red/amber), 'Medium' (yellow), or '-' (no match)

Admin can choose per matched row (via dropdown or button): 'Accept Match' (merge on import — uses transitionToAdult on existing record), 'Skip Match' (create new profile), 'Review Later' (creates new + adds a note to the profile)
Default choice for HIGH confidence: 'Accept Match'. Default for MEDIUM: 'Skip Match'
Import continues via the existing batchImportMutation with the admin's per-row decisions — no new import pipeline needed
Rows with no match (or youth players with age < 18) proceed exactly as before
Show a summary before final import: 'X adult rows will be merged with existing profiles, Y will create new profiles'
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Read admin/player-import/page.tsx fully before modifying. The review table already exists — just add the new column and per-row decision UI. findMatchingYouthProfile from US-P-008 must be built first.

userStories.title: Player Self-Registration via Join Request with Youth Matching
userStories.description: As an adult player who has never received an invite, I want to register myself and join an organisation, so that my existing history is preserved when linked to my youth profile.
userStories.phase: Phase 3: Adult Import & Youth Record Matching
acceptanceCriteria: EXTEND the existing org join request form — add 'I'm a player' option alongside the existing coach/parent role options
When 'player' role is selected: show a DOB field (required when joining as player — used for youth record matching)
On join request submission with player role: backend runs findMatchingYouthProfile with the provided name + DOB
Store the match result (confidence + matched playerIdentityId if any) with the join request record
In the admin's pending requests review UI (existing UI): flag requests where a youth match was found — show 'May match [Name], born [DOB]. Link or create new?'
Admin approval options for player requests with HIGH match: 'Approve & Link to Existing History' or 'Approve & Create New Profile'
'Approve & Link': calls claimYouthProfile(existingPlayerIdentityId, userId) — links user to existing record and transitions to adult if age >= 18
'Approve & Create New': existing approval flow, creates new playerIdentity
On approval (either path): send player a confirmation email via Resend — add sendPlayerJoinApprovalEmail() to packages/backend/convex/utils/email.ts
Player receives email: 'Your request to join [Org] has been approved. You now have access to your player portal.'
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Find the existing join request form and admin review UI. Read how coach/parent join requests are handled to follow the same pattern.

userStories.title: Youth Record Matching on Email Invite
userStories.description: As an admin inviting an adult player by email, I want the system to detect if a youth record exists for this person, so that their history is linked when they accept.
userStories.phase: Phase 3: Adult Import & Youth Record Matching
acceptanceCriteria: EXTEND the existing invite dialog — when DOB is entered alongside email, backend checks findMatchingYouthProfile
If HIGH or MEDIUM match found: show informational note inline in the dialog: 'A youth profile may exist for this person. They'll be linked to their existing history when they accept the invite.'
If no match: proceed with invite as normal, no change to existing flow
Store the matched playerIdentityId in the invitation metadata when a match is found

On invitation acceptance (existing flow via accept-invitation page + onboarding orchestrator): after claimPlayerAccount, if invitation metadata contains a matchedPlayerIdentityId, automatically call transitionToAdult(matchedPlayerIdentityId, userId) if player age >= 18
The inline note is informational only — admin does not need to confirm anything, just see the note
If the invite dialog doesn't currently collect DOB: add DOB as an optional field (only shown for player role invites)
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Find the existing invite dialog. Read how invitations are accepted in the accept-invitation page and onboarding orchestrator. The auto-transition on acceptance must be non-breaking for existing invites that have no matchedPlayerIdentityId.

userStories.title: Phase 3 Adult Import Matching E2E Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Playwright E2E tests for all Phase 3 matching scenarios, so that adult import matching is reliable and regression-free.
userStories.phase: Phase 3: Adult Import & Youth Record Matching
acceptanceCriteria: Create test file: apps/web/uat/tests/adult-import-matching-phase3.spec.ts
Test: manually adding adult player with HIGH confidence match shows blocking dialog
Test: manually adding adult player with no match proceeds directly to creation
Test: 'Link to Existing History' in dialog does not create a new playerIdentity
Test: import CSV with adult row matching existing youth player shows 'High' badge in review table
Test: join request form shows DOB field when 'player' role selected
Test: admin review screen shows match flag for player join request with matching youth record
Manual test 1: Create youth player (DOB = 18 years ago). Try manually adding same person as adult → confirm HIGH match dialog
Manual test 2: In dialog: choose 'Link to Existing' → confirm no new playerIdentity created, existing record now adult
Manual test 3: In dialog: choose 'Create New' → confirm two records now exist
Manual test 4: Upload CSV with adult row matching youth player by name+DOB → confirm 'High' badge in import preview
Manual test 5: Accept match during import → verify no duplicate, existing enrollment updated
Manual test 6: Submit self-registration join request as player with DOB matching youth record → log in as admin → confirm match flag in pending requests
Manual test 7: Invite player by email with DOB matching youth record → confirm informational note appears in invite dialog

userStories.title: Daily Wellness Check Schema & Backend Functions
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need the schema and core CRUD functions for daily wellness checks, so that Phase 4 frontend stories have a backend to work with.
acceptanceCriteria: Add dailyPlayerHealthChecks table to packages/backend/convex/schema.ts with fields: playerIdentityId (id('playerIdentities')), organizationId (string), checkDate (string 'YYYY-MM-DD'), physicalFeeling (number 1-5), energyLevel (number 1-5), mentalWellbeing (number 1-5), muscleRecovery (number 1-5), motivation (number 1-5), cyclePhase (optional union of literals: 'menstruation'|'early_follicular'|'ovulation'|'early_luteal'|'late_luteal'), notes (optional string), submittedAt (number), updatedAt (number)
Add indexes to dailyPlayerHealthChecks: by_player_and_date (['playerIdentityId','checkDate']), by_org_and_date (['organizationId','checkDate']), by_player (['playerIdentityId'])
Add playerHealthConsents table to schema.ts with fields: playerIdentityId (id('playerIdentities')), consentType (literal 'cycle_tracking'), givenAt (number), withdrawnAt (optional number)
Add index to playerHealthConsents: by_player_and_type (['playerIdentityId','consentType'])
Create packages/backend/convex/models/playerHealthChecks.ts with the following functions:

submitDailyHealthCheck mutation: args (playerIdentityId, organizationId, checkDate, physicalFeeling, energyLevel, mentalWellbeing, muscleRecovery, motivation, cyclePhase?, notes?), checks for existing record on same date (by_player_and_date index), throws if already exists (use updateDailyHealthCheck instead), inserts new record
updateDailyHealthCheck mutation: args (same as submit plus the record id), patches existing record, updates updatedAt
getTodayHealthCheck query: args (playerIdentityId, checkDate), returns the record or null — uses by_player_and_date index
getMyHealthChecks query: args (playerIdentityId, startDate, endDate), returns array of records in date range — uses by_player index with date filter

getTeamWellnessSummary query: args (teamId, checkDate, organizationId), batch fetches players on team, batch fetches their health checks for that date, returns per-player summary with average score — NO cycle phase data in response, coaches cannot see it
getOrgWellnessAnalytics query: args (organizationId, startDate, endDate), returns team-level averages and correlation data
grantCycleTrackingConsent mutation: args (playerIdentityId), inserts into playerHealthConsents
withdrawCycleTrackingConsent mutation: args (playerIdentityId), sets withdrawnAt on consent record AND deletes all cyclePhase field data from that player's health check records
getCycleTrackingConsent query: args (playerIdentityId), returns consent record or null
All functions include returns validators
npx -w packages/backend convex codegen passes
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundational story for all Phase 4 frontend stories. Must be completed first. The getTeamWellnessSummary MUST NOT include cyclePhase in the return type — this is a hard privacy requirement.

userStories.title: Daily Wellness Check Submission UI (Player)
userStories.description: As an adult player, I want to quickly rate my wellness each day using emojis, so that my coaches and club have visibility into my readiness and wellbeing.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/health-check/page.tsx
Show all 5 questions on one scrollable screen (not one-at-a-time) for speed
5 questions: 'How does your body feel?', 'How is your energy/sleep?', 'How are you feeling mentally?', 'How are your muscles/soreness?', 'How motivated are you for training today?'
Each question has 5 emoji buttons: 😢 (1 Very Poor, red #ef4444), 😕 (2 Poor, orange #f97316), 😐 (3 Neutral, yellow #eab308), 🙂 (4 Good, light green #86efac), 😁 (5 Great, green #22c55e)
Each emoji button minimum 44×44px touch target for mobile
Selected emoji highlights with the question's accent colour background
Submit button disabled until all 5 core questions are answered
If player.gender === 'female': show cycle phase section below the 5 questions with 'Optional — You don't have to answer this' label
Cycle phase shows as 5 horizontal pills: 'Menstruation (Days 1-5)', 'Early Follicular (Days 6-9)', 'Ovulation (Days 10-14)', 'Early Luteal (Days 15-21)', 'Late Luteal (Days 22-28)'
Cycle phase is ALWAYS optional — submit button does not require it
First time a female player taps any cycle phase pill: intercept with GDPR consent modal BEFORE recording selection (see US-P-013b)
On submit: calls submitDailyHealthCheck mutation
If today's check already submitted: show the existing answers pre-selected, submit button changes to 'Update', calls updateDailyHealthCheck
Success toast: 'Wellness check submitted ✓'
Streak indicator below submit button: 'You've checked in X days in a row 🔥' — query consecutive days from getMyHealthChecks
Mobile-first layout at 375px, works on desktop too
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: US-P-011-SCHEMA must be complete first. The cycle phase GDPR modal (US-P-013b) is a dependency for the cycle phase section. If US-P-013b isn't done, show the cycle phase section but stub out the GDPR check as a TODO.

userStories.title: Under-18 Player Health Check Access
userStories.description: As a youth player with their own account, I want to submit my daily wellness check, so that my parents and coaches can monitor my wellbeing.
acceptanceCriteria: The health-check page (US-P-011) is accessible from the player portal for both adult AND youth players who have claimed their account
Add a 'Youth Account' badge visible in the player portal header for users with playerType === 'youth'

Parent must explicitly enable under-18 player portal access: add a toggle in parent portal settings for each child (toggle in parent portal settings page for that child: 'Allow [Name] to access their own player portal')
If parent has NOT enabled access: youth player sees the player portal with a 'Your parent hasn't enabled your player access yet' message
Parent can VIEW their child's wellness submissions read-only in parent portal: add 'Wellness' tab to the child's detail view in the parent portal
Parent wellness tab shows check-in history with dates and the 5 scores — read-only, no ability to submit on child's behalf
Parent DOES NOT receive alerts or notifications based on wellness scores
When player turns 18 and claims adult account: parent access to wellness data is revoked (automatic — based on playerType change to adult)

Cycle phase section does NOT appear for under-18 players (GDPR — under-18 cannot consent to special category data collection without parental consent — simplest approach: hide entirely for youth accounts)
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Read the parent portal settings page to find where to add the toggle. The parent portal Wellness tab is read-only view only.

userStories.title: Coach Team Wellness Dashboard
userStories.description: As a coach, I want to see my players' daily wellness scores, so that I can adapt training and identify players who need support.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Team Wellness' section to coach Team Hub — extend the existing health-safety-widget.tsx pattern
Section header shows today's check-in completion rate: 'X/Y players checked in today'
Player list showing each player's name + average wellness score (average of the 5 dimensions) as a colour-coded badge: red (avg < 2), orange (avg 2-2.9), yellow (avg 3-3.9), green (avg >= 4)
Players with ANY single dimension score <= 2 get an alert icon (⚠️) next to their name
Tapping/clicking a player row expands to show their individual 5-dimension breakdown for today (or most recent check)
7-day rolling wellness trend chart per player (small sparkline or bar chart) — uses Recharts wrapped in ResponsiveContainer
CYCLE PHASE IS NEVER SHOWN TO COACHES — the getTeamWellnessSummary query returns aggregate scores only, cyclePhase field excluded from response
If no players have submitted today: show 'No wellness check-ins yet today' empty state
Data fetched via getTeamWellnessSummary(teamId, checkDate, organizationId) query
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Find health-safety-widget.tsx in the coach pages and extend it. getTeamWellnessSummary is built in US-P-011-SCHEMA. Critical: the backend query must never return cyclePhase data — this is enforced at the query level.

userStories.title: GDPR Consent Flow for Menstrual Cycle Tracking
userStories.description: As a female player, I want to be clearly informed about what menstrual cycle data is collected and give explicit consent, so that my sensitive health data is handled in accordance with GDPR.
acceptanceCriteria: Before a female player can interact with the cycle phase section for the first time, a modal intercepts and must be completed
Check consent status via getCycleTrackingConsent(playerIdentityId) — if consent record exists with no withdrawnAt, show the section directly; if no consent or withdrawn, show consent modal first
Consent modal title: 'Menstrual Cycle Tracking'

Consent modal body (plain language): explain what is collected (cycle phase only, not dates), why (sports performance and injury risk analysis), who sees it (you and authorised medical staff at [org name] only — coaches do NOT see this), how long it's kept (up to 18 months), and that you can withdraw consent at any time
Include a link to the PlayerARC privacy policy in the modal
Single explicit opt-in checkbox: 'I consent to PlayerARC storing my menstrual cycle phase data for sports performance analysis. I can withdraw this consent at any time in Settings.'
Checkbox is NOT pre-ticked
Two buttons: 'I Consent' (enabled only when checkbox ticked) and 'Skip / No Thanks'
On 'I Consent': calls grantCycleTrackingConsent(playerIdentityId), then shows cycle phase section
On 'Skip / No Thanks': permanently hides cycle phase section (stores a preference, does not create consent record)
In player settings page (/player/settings): add 'Menstrual Cycle Tracking' toggle showing current consent status

Toggling OFF the settings toggle calls withdrawCycleTrackingConsent(playerIdentityId) which sets withdrawnAt on the consent record AND deletes all cyclePhase field values from past health check records for this player
Toggling ON in settings shows the same consent modal again
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: This is GDPR Article 9 Special Category data. The consent must be separate, explicit, and not pre-ticked. The withdrawCycleTrackingConsent mutation in US-P-011-SCHEMA handles data deletion. Create the /player/settings page if it doesn't exist.

userStories.title: Admin Wellness Reminder Configuration
userStories.description: As an org admin, I want to configure wellness check reminders for my organisation, so that players are prompted to check in at the right time.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Wellness Reminders' section to org admin settings page
Master toggle: 'Enable wellness check reminders' (on/off) — when off, no reminders sent to anyone
When enabled: frequency options (radio buttons): 'Daily', 'Match days only', 'Training days only'
When enabled: reminder type (checkboxes): 'In-app notification', 'Email', or both
When enabled: reminder sends only to players who haven't submitted a check for today (checked via by_player_and_date index — do not send duplicate reminders)
Separate section: 'Low Score Alerts' — toggle to enable admin/medical staff alerts when a player scores <= 2 on any dimension
Low score alert: recipient options (who gets notified): 'Org admins', 'Medical staff only', or 'Both'
Low score alerts are NEVER sent to coaches (coaches view data voluntarily on their dashboard)
Store reminder settings in org settings — add fields to the org settings storage (find existing pattern for org settings)
A scheduled Convex cron job (or action triggered by mutation) handles sending the reminders at the configured frequency
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Find how org settings are stored currently (likely in the organization record or a separate orgSettings table). Follow that pattern. The reminder cron logic should live in packages/backend/convex/jobs/ directory.

userStories.title: Admin Wellness & Injury Correlation Analytics
userStories.description: As an org admin or medical staff member, I want to see wellness data alongside injury records, so that I can identify patterns and reduce injury risk.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Player Wellness' tab/section to admin analytics — can be a new page or added to existing admin analytics if it exists
Team-level view: average daily wellness scores for each team, shown as a time series line chart (Recharts LineChart, ResponsiveContainer)
Correlation view: list of players who had 3+ consecutive days with average wellness score < 3, alongside any injuries reported in that same period
Correlation view is informational — shows: player name, low wellness period (dates), any injuries in that period, suggesting correlation
Cycle phase analysis section (visible only to admins with medical access role): aggregate heatmap or bar chart of injury occurrences grouped by cycle phase across all female players in the org
Cycle phase analysis clearly labelled 'Medical Staff Access Only'
Date range filter: start date and end date pickers, affects all charts and tables on the page
CSV export button: exports all wellness data for the selected date range as CSV (player name, date, 5 scores, cycle phase if user has medical access)
CSV export escapes commas in data (replace commas with semicolons in values)
CSV export uses Blob + URL.createObjectURL + link.download pattern
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: getOrgWellnessAnalytics query from US-P-011-SCHEMA provides the data. Follow the Recharts patterns already established in the injury analytics pages (ResponsiveContainer, grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2).

userStories.title: Phase 4 Daily Wellness E2E Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Playwright E2E tests for the daily wellness check feature, so that submission, editing, visibility rules, and GDPR consent all work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test file: apps/web/uat/tests/daily-wellness-phase4.spec.ts
Test: wellness check page shows 5 questions with 5 emoji buttons each
Test: submit button disabled until all 5 answered
Test: submit with all 5 answered shows success toast
Test: revisiting same day shows existing answers pre-selected and 'Update' button
Test: cycle phase section hidden for male player
Test: GDPR consent modal appears on first cycle phase interaction for female player
Test: cycle phase section hidden after consent withdrawal
Test: coach wellness dashboard shows today's completion count
Test: cycle phase data does NOT appear in coach view
Manual test 1: Log in as adult female player → navigate to Daily Wellness → confirm 5 questions with emoji buttons
Manual test 2: Tap emoji for each question → confirm selected highlights in colour
Manual test 3: Submit all 5 → confirm success toast and submission in DB
Manual test 4: Return same day → confirm current answers shown, 'Update' button visible
Manual test 5: Edit one answer → update → confirm new answer persisted
Manual test 6: Submit with only 4 answered → confirm submit button disabled
Manual test 7: Female player → scroll to cycle phase section → confirm 'Optional' label
Manual test 8: First tap on cycle phase → confirm GDPR consent modal with unticked checkbox
Manual test 9: Accept consent → select cycle phase → submit → confirm cycle phase in DB
Manual test 10: Go to Settings → toggle off cycle tracking → confirm withdrawnAt set and all cyclePhase fields null in past records
Manual test 11: Log in as male player → confirm cycle phase section NOT shown
Manual test 12: Log in as coach → Team Wellness widget shows today's completion rate and player wellness badges
Manual test 13: Confirm coaches see NO cycle phase data
Manual test 14: Log in as org admin → navigate to wellness analytics → confirm org-wide charts visible
Manual test 15: Under-18 player: parent enables access → player submits wellness → log in as parent → confirm wellness visible read-only in parent portal
Manual test 16: Disable wellness reminders in admin settings → enable with daily frequency → confirm in-app notification sent to players
userStories.notes: All Phase 4 stories (US-P-011-SCHEMA through US-P-013c) must be complete before this UAT story.

userStories.title: My Progress — Player View of Sport Passports
userStories.description: As an adult player, I want to see my sport passport ratings and assessment history, so that I can track my own development.
userStories.phase: Phase 5: Full Player Portal Remaining Sections
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/progress/page.tsx
Fetch own passports via getPassportsForPlayer(playerIdentityId) — find the correct existing query for this
If multiple sports: show tab/pill switcher at top to select between sports
Each sport shows skill ratings (technical, tactical, physical, mental) as read-only display — same visual as coach view but with no edit controls
Show trend arrows (↑ ↓ =) next to each rating based on most recent vs previous assessment
Assessment history timeline below ratings: list of assessment dates with the summary score for each
Player CANNOT edit their own ratings — no edit buttons, inputs are display-only. Show a note: 'Ratings are set by your coach'
Player CAN add their own notes: 'My Notes' section with a textarea that saves via a player notes mutation — find the existing player notes pattern on the passport
Empty state: 'No passport assessments yet. Your coach will complete your first assessment.' if no passports
Mobile responsive at 375px
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Find the existing getPassportsForPlayer query or equivalent. Check sportPassports and sportPassportAssessments tables in the schema. Mirror the coach's view but remove all edit capabilities.

userStories.title: My Passport Sharing Management (Player Controls Own Sharing)
userStories.description: As an adult player, I want to control who can see my sports passport, so that I can share my data with other clubs when I choose.
userStories.phase: Phase 5: Full Player Portal Remaining Sections
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/sharing/page.tsx
Mirror the existing parent sharing UI at apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/parents/sharing/page.tsx — same controls and patterns
Player can enable/disable passport sharing (toggle) — same backend as parent sharing
Player can approve or deny sharing enquiries from other orgs
When a player claims their account (US-P-006): sharing control transfers from guardian to player. Guardian's sharing settings for this player should be revoked/transferred.
Add a migration or mutation: after claimPlayerAccount succeeds, update passportSharing records for this playerIdentityId to transfer ownership from guardian to player userId
If guardian had previously shared the passport: player sees it is currently shared and can choose to disable
Empty state: 'No sharing requests yet. Enable sharing to let other clubs view your passport.'
Mobile responsive at 375px
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Read apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/parents/sharing/page.tsx fully before implementing. The passportSharing table and backend queries already exist for parents — reuse them. The ownership transfer on claim is the new part.

userStories.title: My Injuries — Player Self-View and Self-Report
userStories.description: As an adult player, I want to view and self-report my own injuries, so that I can track my recovery and keep my club informed.
userStories.phase: Phase 5: Full Player Portal Remaining Sections
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/injuries/page.tsx
Fetch own injury history — find the existing getInjuriesForPlayer or equivalent query (playerInjuries table)
Show list of injuries: body part, injury type, severity, date occurred, current status (active/recovering/cleared/healed)
Historic (coach-reported) injuries: display read-only with 'Reported by coach' badge
Player-reported injuries: display with 'You reported this' badge
Current active injury (if any): show prominently at top with status, expected return date, recovery milestones

'Report New Injury' button opens a form dialog with fields: body part (dropdown), injury type (dropdown or text), severity (minor/moderate/severe), date occurred, occurred during (training/match/other), notes
On submit: creates injury with reportedByRole set to 'player' (check playerInjuries schema for this field)
After self-report: injury appears in coach's injury view for that player with 'Player-reported' badge
Empty state: 'No injuries on record. Stay healthy!' if no injuries
Mobile responsive at 375px
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Check packages/backend/convex/models/playerInjuries.ts for the existing queries and mutations. The reportedByRole field should already exist on playerInjuries — verify in schema.ts. Follow the coach injury reporting pattern.

userStories.title: Phase 5 Player Portal Sections E2E Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Playwright E2E tests for the full player portal sections, so that progress, sharing, and injury features work end-to-end.
userStories.phase: Phase 5: Full Player Portal Remaining Sections
acceptanceCriteria: Create test file: apps/web/uat/tests/player-portal-phase5.spec.ts
Test: My Progress shows sport passport ratings with read-only display
Test: My Progress — player cannot edit skill ratings (no edit buttons present)
Test: My Passport Sharing — sharing toggle changes state in DB
Test: My Injuries — displays existing injury list
Test: My Injuries — self-report form creates new injury with reportedByRole 'player'
Manual test 1: Log in as adult player with sport passport → navigate to My Progress → confirm ratings visible with sport tabs
Manual test 2: Confirm player CANNOT edit skill ratings (no edit controls visible)
Manual test 3: Confirm player CAN add a personal note to passport notes section
Manual test 4: Navigate to My Passport Sharing → toggle sharing on → confirm sharing enabled in DB
Manual test 5: Navigate to My Injuries → confirm existing injuries shown
Manual test 6: Click 'Report New Injury' → fill fields → submit → confirm injury created with reportedByRole 'player'
Manual test 7: New injury appears in list with 'You reported this' badge
Manual test 8: Log in as coach → confirm the player-reported injury appears in coach injury view with 'Player-reported' badge
userStories.notes: US-P-015, US-P-016, and US-P-017 must all be complete before this UAT story.

userStories.title: My Coach Feedback & AI Summaries (Player View)
userStories.description: As an adult player, I want to see the feedback and AI summaries my coaches have chosen to share with me so that I can understand my development from my coach's perspective.
userStories.phase: Phase 5: Full Player Portal Remaining Sections
acceptanceCriteria: Route: /orgs/[orgId]/player/feedback
Backend query returns only publicSummary fields from coachParentSummaries where status is approved/auto_approved/delivered/viewed — privateInsight is NEVER fetched or returned
Chronological feed newest first: coach name, date, AI summary text, sensitivity badge (normal/injury/behavior)
Player can acknowledge a summary (existing acknowledge mutation) — 'Acknowledged ✓' state shown after
All three sensitivity categories visible — this is the player's own development data
Empty state: 'Your coaches haven't shared any feedback with you yet.'
Mobile-first: 375px minimum width
See phase file adult-player-phase5-portal-sections.prd.json for full detail
userStories.notes: Read coachParentSummaries.ts fully. privateInsight is NEVER shown. publicSummary is the AI-generated text visible after coach approval.

userStories.title: Primary Role & Default Dashboard Setting
userStories.description: As a user with multiple roles, I want to set which role I land on after logging in so that my most important dashboard is immediately accessible.
acceptanceCriteria: My Roles section in account settings shows all held functional roles
Set as primary action updates primaryFunctionalRole on member record
On next login: routing reads primaryFunctionalRole for default dashboard
Does not log user out mid-session
See phase file adult-player-phase6-multi-role.prd.json for full detail
userStories.notes: primaryFunctionalRole already exists on member schema. Read org-role-switcher.tsx before implementing.

userStories.title: Role Context Badge in Navigation
userStories.description: As a user with multiple roles, I want to always know which role I am currently acting as so that I cannot take actions in the wrong context.
acceptanceCriteria: Persistent role context badge in page header: 'Acting as: [Role]'
Only shown for users with 2+ roles
Colour-coded by role using org theme vars
Visible on mobile without opening a menu
See phase file adult-player-phase6-multi-role.prd.json for full detail
userStories.notes: Read org-role-switcher.tsx and layout files before implementing.

userStories.title: Adding Player Role to an Existing Account
userStories.description: As an existing platform member, I want to register myself as a player without creating a new account.
acceptanceCriteria: My Roles settings section includes 'Register as a player' option (only if not already a player)
Form collects DOB and optional team selection
Runs findMatchingYouthProfile on submit; HIGH match → admin review before linking
Creates pending playerIdentity + enrollment; admin approves in existing UI
No duplicate user account created
See phase file adult-player-phase6-multi-role.prd.json for full detail
userStories.notes: findMatchingYouthProfile is from Phase 3 — reuse it.

userStories.title: Cross-Role Permission Scenarios
userStories.description: As a user with multiple roles, I want the platform to handle cross-role edge cases correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Coach cannot self-assess own player profile (actions disabled with tooltip)
Parent and player portals are entirely separate contexts
Admin modifying own player record: confirmation dialog required
See phase file adult-player-phase6-multi-role.prd.json for full detail
userStories.notes: Guard rails only — not major feature work.

userStories.title: Phase 6 Multi-Role E2E Tests
userStories.description: Playwright E2E tests for all Phase 6 multi-role stories.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: apps/web/uat/tests/multi-role-phase6.spec.ts
See phase file adult-player-phase6-multi-role.prd.json for full test list
userStories.notes: All Phase 6 stories must be complete before UAT.

userStories.title: Backend: parentChildAuthorizations Schema & Queries
userStories.description: Backend schemas and queries for the child access authorization system.
acceptanceCriteria: Add parentChildAuthorizations table to schema.ts (see PRD for full schema)
Add restrictChildView field to relevant note/insight table
Implement grantChildAccess, revokeChildAccess, getChildAuthorization, getChildrenForParent, updateChildAccessToggles, getRestrictedNotes, setNoteChildRestriction mutations/queries
Age check in grantChildAccess: reject if child age < 13
See phase file adult-player-phase7-child-authorization.prd.json for full detail
userStories.notes: Must be first story in Phase 7. Add to schema.ts and run codegen before writing queries.

userStories.title: Parent Grants Child Platform Access
userStories.description: As a parent, I want to grant my under-18 child controlled access to their player development data.
acceptanceCriteria: Grant Player Access section in parent portal on child's profile page
Access level selector: View Only (ages 13–15) / View + Interact (ages 16–17)
Granular toggles: coach feedback, voice notes, development goals, assessments, wellness
Age < 13 blocked at UI and backend
Sends invite email to child on first grant
Second parent/guardian sees unified access level — all changes logged in changeLog
See phase file adult-player-phase7-child-authorization.prd.json for full detail
userStories.notes: US-P-023 (backend) must be complete first.

userStories.title: Child Account Creation & Onboarding
userStories.description: As a child player, I want to set up my own account after my parent has granted me access.
acceptanceCriteria: Public route /child-account-setup?token=xxx (similar to Phase 2 /claim-account)
DOB check at account setup: block if under 13
child_account_setup onboarding orchestrator task type
Child lands on player portal with 'Youth Account' badge after setup
See phase file adult-player-phase7-child-authorization.prd.json for full detail
userStories.notes: Follow Phase 2 /claim-account pattern closely.

userStories.title: Child Player Dashboard — View Only Mode
userStories.description: As a child player with View Only access (ages 13–15), I want to see my sports development data in read-only format.
acceptanceCriteria: Uses same /player/ portal from Phase 1 with Youth Account badge
All fields read-only, all edit actions disabled
Content gated by granular toggles from parentChildAuthorizations
Never shows: medical info, emergency contacts, fees, restrictChildView notes, privateInsight
Cycle phase section never shown for under-18 players
See phase file adult-player-phase7-child-authorization.prd.json for full detail
userStories.notes: Implement shared useChildAccess() hook to centralize access level checking.

userStories.title: Child View + Interact Mode (Ages 16–17)
userStories.description: As a child player with View + Interact access, I want to set personal goals and respond to coach feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Personal development goals: child can add own goals (setByRole: 'player'), labelled 'My Goal'
Notes on coach feedback: child can append text response, visible to parent and coach
Acknowledge coach messages
See phase file adult-player-phase7-child-authorization.prd.json for full detail
userStories.notes: Builds on View Only mode — all View Only content applies.

userStories.title: Coach Parent-Only Note Filtering
userStories.description: As a coach, I want to mark certain notes as parent-only so the child never sees them.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle on voice note/insight creation: 'Restrict from child view — Parent and coach only'
Notes with restrictChildView: true excluded from child's portal (silently — no indicator)
Existing notes default to false (no behaviour change)
See phase file adult-player-phase7-child-authorization.prd.json for full detail
userStories.notes: restrictChildView field added in US-P-023. This story adds the coach UI toggle.

userStories.title: 30-Day and 7-Day Pre-Birthday Advance Notifications
userStories.description: As a parent and player approaching 18, I want advance notification of the upcoming full account transition.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend existing graduation cron to check players turning 18 in exactly 30 and 7 days
Notification types: age_transition_30_days and age_transition_7_days
30-day notification to parent and child (if has account)
Guard against duplicate notifications
See phase file adult-player-phase7-child-authorization.prd.json for full detail
userStories.notes: Read graduation cron fully before modifying. Additive only — Phase 2 birthday handling unchanged.

userStories.title: Phase 7 Child Authorization E2E Tests
userStories.description: Playwright E2E tests for the full child authorization system.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: apps/web/uat/tests/child-authorization-phase7.spec.ts
See phase file adult-player-phase7-child-authorization.prd.json for full test list
userStories.notes: All Phase 7 stories must be complete before UAT.
